zh.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland是William Holland的兒子，於大約1590年出生在英國。他於1610年之前與Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1659年1月17日之前在英國米德萊可聖 澤普爾可 紐蓋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland是William Holland的儿子，于大约1590年出生在英国。他于1610年之前与Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1659年1月17日之前在英国米德萊可聖 澤普爾可 紐蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howland
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland was born about 1590, in England as the son of William Holland. He married Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland before 1610. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died before 17 January 1659, in St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland est né environ 1590, à Angleterre, fils de William Holland. Il a épousé Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland avant 1610. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 17 janvier 1659, à St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland wurde um 1590 in England, als Sohn von William Holland geboren. Er hat vor 1610 Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 17. Januar 1659 in St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland è nato il circa 1590 nel England come figlio di William Holland. Lui sposato Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland prima del 1610. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 17 gennaio 1659, nel St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joseph Hollandは約1590年にイギリスでWilliam Hollandの息子として生まれた。1610年以前にUnknown 1st wife of Joseph Hollandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1659年1月17日以前にイギリスミッドレセックススト セプウルチア ニューガトで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland(은)는 대략 1590년 영국에서 William Holland의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1610년 이전 Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1659년 1월 17일 이전 영국 미들레세스 스트 세풀크르 뉴가트에서 사망했다.

mn.about 1590–before 17 January 1659: ойролцоогоор 1590 он–1659 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 17–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland nasceu aproximadamente 1590, em Inglaterra filho de William Holland. Ele casou-se com Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland antes 1610. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 17 de janeiro de 1659, em St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland родился около 1590 г. в местности Англия. Его отца звали William Holland. Он женился на женщине по имени Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland перед 1610 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 17 январь 1659 г. в местности ст сепулчр нюгэит, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joseph Holland nació aproximadamente 1590, en Inglaterra como hijo de William Holland. Se casó con Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland antes de 1610. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 17 de enero de 1659, en St Sepulchre Newgate, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
